100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On April 13, 1926, the British government faced mounting pressure as a general strike loomed, with labor unrest spreading across the nation. In the United States, a notable trial continued with the scandalous testimony of a former bootlegger shedding light on organized crime. Meanwhile, in aviation news, Charles Lindbergh's upcoming transatlantic flight plans stirred excitement and anticipation among the public.
